text.title: Agentic Commerce Model
text.summary: AI agents autonomously complete purchase flows — comparing options, selecting providers, executing transactions. Evaluation factors differ fundamentally from traditional SEO: earned media, review quality, entity authority, and API availability matter more than keywords.

section.title: What Agentic Commerce Is
section.p: AI agents autonomously complete entire purchase flows: compare options across providers, make recommendations based on trust signals, and execute transactions without human search. The traditional user journey (search → click → browse → buy) collapses to (user intent → AI agent → confirm).

example.code: Traditional: User → "plumber near me" → Google results → click → browse → book Agentic: User → "fix my sink this afternoon" → AI selects provider → confirm booking

rule.statement: Optimize for the factors AI agents weight when selecting providers: earned media (high), review quality (high), entity authority (high), cross-platform consistency (medium), structured data (medium), API availability (medium).
rule.rationale: These factors are harder to game than keywords. Keyword stuffing, bought backlinks, and thin content are detectable by AI systems that cross-reference multiple sources. Genuine authority requires long-term investment — which also means it is a more durable competitive advantage.

list.item: Traditional SEO: optimize for keywords and backlinks. Measurable in weeks.
Agentic commerce: optimize for trust and authority. Measurable in months to years.
Traditional SEO: one primary source (Google). Agentic: multiple AI systems cross-referencing each other.
